figures.caption: Figure 1: Forward verification of the trained 1D inverse PINN at the representative configuration (Nobs = 50, no noise, seed 0). Top row: depth and velocity profiles, with the analytical reference (dashed), the network prediction (solid), and the observation points (markers). Middle row: pointwise absolute error. Bottom row: training-loss history and the friction-coefficient trajectory, showing the Phase A plateau and the Phase B descent.

Figure 2: 1D MacDonald benchmark: dependence of recovered friction coefficient (left) and depth-field reconstruction error (right) on the number of observations at zero noise. Markers and error bars indicate mean and standard deviation over five seeds; grey dots show individual seed values.

Figure 3: 1D MacDonald benchmark: (a) relative error in the recovered friction coefficient as a function of observation noise level, for each observation count. Markers and error bars indicate mean and standard deviation over five seeds; (b) observation-type ablation at Nobs = 20 and no noise. Bars show seed-mean relative error in the recovered friction coefficient with standard-deviation error bars over five seeds.

Figure 4: 1D MacDonald benchmark: convergence trajectories of the Manning coefficient across five seeds at Nobs = 20 and no noise. The vertical dotted line marks the transition from Phase A to Phase B.

Figure 6: Forward verification of the trained 2D inverse PINN at the representative configuration (Nobs = 50, no noise, seed 0). Top row: predicted depth, streamwise velocity, and transverse velocity fields with observation locations overlaid. Middle row: reference fields from the finite-volume solver. Bottom row: absolute pointwise error.

Figure 7: 2D sloped-channel benchmark: training diagnostics at the representative configuration. Left: observation and physics loss as a function of epoch, with the Phase A to Phase B transition marked by the vertical dotted line. Right: convergence trajectory of the Manning coefficient toward its target value.

Figure 8: 2D sloped-channel benchmark: (a) dependence of the recovered friction coefficient on the number of observations at zero noise. Markers and error bars show mean and standard deviation over five seeds; grey dots show individual seed values. (b) relative error in the recovered friction coefficient as a function of observation noise level, for each observation count. Markers and error bars show mean and standard deviation over five seeds.
Figure 9: 2D sloped-channel benchmark: relative error in the recovered friction coefficient across the full sparsity–noise grid. Cell values are seed-mean relative errors in percent.
Table 1: Recovered Manning coefficient and depth-field accuracy at zero noise for both benchmarks. Statistics are mean ± standard deviation over five seeds. Target value: ntrue = 0.020.

Figure 10: 2D sloped-channel benchmark: convergence trajectories of the Manning coefficient across five seeds at Nobs = 20 and no noise. The vertical dotted line marks the transition from Phase A to Phase B.
